meaning: 萨洛尼卡[希腊中北部港市](临萨洛尼卡湾) ;萨洛尼卡湾(靠希腊中北岸)
examples: Mr Sarkozy, whose maternal grandfather was a Jew from Salonika, is the first French president born after the war.
Mind on the job: Silva (right) in training with Carlos Tevez ahead of Thursday's Europa League clash against Aris Salonika at eastlands.
City beat Aris Salonika 3-0 to set up a meeting with the 13-time Ukrainian champions, who are also three-time European Cup semi-finalists.
Two decades later, the old, multi-ethnic character of Salonika took a final, devastating blow.
Salonika's new but unfinished sewage-treatment plant wafts its fragrance across the otherwise attractive waterfront.
